items.title: Andrew and Tristan Tate's renewed defamation claim names Australian YouTuber

children.content: An Adelaide YouTuber has been identified in a renewed defamation lawsuit self-described misogynist Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan have lodged with a US court against some of their online critics.

gallery.altText: Andrew Tate wearing sunglasses walks with brother Tristan

thumbnail.altText: Andrew Tate wearing sunglasses walks with brother Tristan

items.title: Octopus species mysteriously disappears from Spencer Gulf

children.content: The once-abundant pale octopus has not been caught in lower Spencer Gulf for more than a year, and what happened to the species remains a mystery.

image.altText: An orange octopus on a blue fishing bin lip

items.title: 'No rush': Crows won't put timeline on Dawson's return to play

children.content: Adelaide coach Matthew Nicks says there will be no rush for captain Jordan Dawson to return to playing after the death of his brother last week.

image.altText: The Adelaide Oval big screen displays a picture of the Dawson family with a message of support as the crowd applauds

items.title: Crows sensationally down Saints after emotional scenes at Adelaide Oval

children.content: The Crows hold on by one point in a thriller at Adelaide Oval, as the club pays tribute to grieving skipper Jordan Dawson.

image.altText: Two AFL players in stripes hug and celebrate a goal

items.title: LIV Golf facing fight to 'keep going', CEO admits in deleted interview

children.content: The future of LIV Golf remains in doubt as chief executive Scott O'Neil admits in an interview that the league is "funded through the season" but work is required beyond that "to keep us going".

image.altText: Scott O'Neil walks on the first tee box in front of LIV Golf signage

items.title: A blunder in Narungga leaves SA's electoral authority in damage control

children.content: The 2026 state election has been a reputational disaster for South Australia's electoral authority. Its blunder in Narungga was the "cherry on top".

articles.title: Missing votes counted in key SA seat see One Nation margin increase

children.content: One Nation has retained the South Australian seat of Narungga, following a count of ballots that were missed on election day.

image.altText: One Nation's Chantelle Thomas at a media conference.

articles.title: Uncovered ballots prompt new count in key SA seat won by One Nation

children.content: The Electoral Commission of South Australia has uncovered more than 600 ballot papers that were not counted in the recent state election, including dozens from the closely contested seat of Narungga on the Yorke Peninsula.

image.altText: A composite image of two women with blonde hair

gallery.altText: A hand puts a lower house vote into a sealed ballot box as another person points to the top of the box

thumbnail.altText: A hand puts a lower house vote into a sealed ballot box as another person points to the top of the box

items.title: 'High-octane world' of drifting told through music in new concerto

children.content: Motorsport and orchestral music are coming together in a premiere in Adelaide this weekend.

image.altText: A clarinetist rehearsing a concerto.

items.title: Fishers deploy 'ninja tactics' to outsmart fish-stealing seals

children.content: Commercial fishers question their future as they compete with ravenous long-nosed seals that continue to rip man-sized holes in fish nets in South Australia's Coorong.

image.altText: A close up of the face of a wet seal resting on a log

items.title: This type of croquet is a far cry from the Alice in Wonderland version

children.content: Golf croquet is helping people reconnect with others through social competition, morning tea and laughs.

gallery.altText: Elderly woman sitting on a stool in garden holding a purple croquet mallet

thumbnail.altText: Elderly woman sitting on a stool in garden holding a purple croquet mallet

items.title: Bomb squad removes 'volatile' explosives from Adelaide home, police say

children.content: Police say the bomb squad has now safely removed chemicals from the suburban Adelaide home of a 55-year-old man who has been charged with explosives offences, but say the matter is not terror-related.

image.altText: The South Australia Police logo on a building.

items.title: Bandicoots bounce back in Brown Hill Creek as team works towards 'superhighway'

children.content: A small colony of endangered brown bandicoots just south of Adelaide continue to breed as conservationists hope to connect them with other populations.

image.altText: Southern brown bandicoot, Isoodon obesulus obesulus

items.title: BreastScreen SA did not get ethics approval or consent before research, ombudsman finds

children.content: South Australia's public breast cancer screening service conducted research without ethics approval or consent from almost 100 patients, who it asked to do a second breast needle biopsy, prompting the service to "unreservedly" apologise.

gallery.altText: The office of BreastScreen SA in Adelaide.

thumbnail.altText: The office of BreastScreen SA in Adelaide.

items.title: Airport workers angered by surprise Qantas route cuts

children.content: Qantas's decision to cut one regional South Australian route and suspend four others came as a shock to workers at regional airports.

image.altText: A QantasLink plane on the tarmac

items.title: 'Attention-seeking' man allegedly targeted police, defence in 'cybercrime spree'

children.content: A 22-year-old Adelaide man who allegedly went on a "cybercrime spree", targeting several government departments and a courthouse, will remain behind bars until at the least the end of the year.

image.altText: Close up image of the face of a man with Asian heritage

items.title: SA government has 'assurances' from LIV Golf as course upgrade under fresh scrutiny

children.content: The South Australian government has reaffirmed its commitment to a $45 million upgrade of the North Adelaide public golf course to host LIV Golf in 2028, and has "assurances" about the tour's future.

image.altText: Two flags on a golf course with large buildings from the city in the background.

items.title: SA government apologises to prisoner denied abortion medication

children.content: The South Australian ombudsman calls for changes to medical monitoring at the Adelaide Women's Prison after a female prisoner was denied a medical abortion, which the health minister says is unacceptable.

image.altText: A building surrounded by a fence and trees

items.title: The myths of motherhood and the damage they do

children.content: I traced my family's maternal line from a home for abandoned children in Italy to my own wheelchair, confronting the myths we cling to about what makes a good mother — and the damage they do.

gallery.altText: Silhouette of a mother and young child facing away from each other with an image of an old Italian city in the background

thumbnail.altText: Silhouette of a mother and young child facing away from each other with an image of an old Italian city in the background

items.title: Out at sea, 35 million barrels of oil buffer Japan from the fuel crisis

children.content: Japan has oil stockpile sites littered around the country, and as the world faces a fuel crisis, neighbours in Asia have been turning to Tokyo for assistance.

image.altText: An aerial view shows eight barges floating in the water just off Japan.

items.title: Japan, Australia sign deal for production of first three frigates

children.content: Defence Minister Richard Marles and his Japanese counterpart, Koizumi Shinjirō, signed the deal aboard one of the warships docked in Melbourne.

image.altText: Richard Marles and Koizumi Shinjiro sit side by side onboard a warship signing documents.

items.title: The mystery of an outback 'catch and kill' squad during WWII has been solved

children.content: In the 1940s, a shadowy military unit was assembled to catch and kill Japanese soldiers on Australia's remote northern coast. The identity of the so-called Solomons Six was a mystery — until now.

gallery.altText: Four back and white headshots of men dressed in army uniform.
A black and white photo of a large group of men standing to attention in the bush
A black and white photo of a group of men studying pictures of aeroplanes on the deck of a boat.

thumbnail.altText: Four back and white headshots of men dressed in army uniform.
A black and white photo of a large group of men standing to attention in the bush
A black and white photo of a group of men studying pictures of aeroplanes on the deck of a boat.

items.title: The quiet disappearance of Australia's rainfall observation network

children.content: For more than 60 years, Frank Pritchard has diligently checked the weather, adding to a record that stretches back more than a century. But he's part of a quietly shrinking network of observers around Australia. Without it, scientists say we risk missing out on important information that could help improve weather forecast models and climate projections.

image.altText: An older man with balding white hair holds up a rain gauge.

items.title: 'It's a free-for-all': Fiji 'ground zero' for transnational drug-smuggling trade

children.content: Two men have told Foreign Correspondent how they were unwittingly drawn into Fiji's largest-ever meth importation.

image.altText: A marina from above.

items.title: 'Lightning rod for controversy': History of world's first sex worker statue

children.content: During her short residency in Sydney, the statue of sex worker Joy had many detractors and was even vandalised. Thirty years later, she has returned, with her supporters hoping she will dismantle stereotypes that still plague the industry.

items.title: There's a confidence in you I admire. Let's play, HARD!

children.content: Righteo champ, it's time to put down your toast and impress with your trivia prowess. Let's play, HARD!

image.altText: Tom Gleeson on the set of Hard Quiz gesturing behind him with a serious expression.

items.title: Doing disco or school pick-up, Jessie Ware is having more fun than ever

children.content: Whether making opulent, grown-up disco-pop or doing school pick-up, the glam British artist is having more fun than ever.

image.altText: Middle-aged brunette woman wearing fashionable skirt, bra & chain top poses with right hand upright

items.title: The show the ABC didn't 'get' and Channel 10 cancelled twice

children.content: On April 19, 1999, comedy news game show Good News Week premiered, hosted by Paul McDermott, sparking nine seasons, a Gold Logie nomination and some first-chances for comedians.

gallery.altText: Paul McDermott, 30s, Julie McCrossin, 40s, and Mikey Robins, 30s, pose together on a stage littered with papers.

thumbnail.altText: Paul McDermott, 30s, Julie McCrossin, 40s, and Mikey Robins, 30s, pose together on a stage littered with papers.

avatar.alt: Hannah Story, a blonde woman sitting outside smiles. A dog is at her feet, and she wears a T-shirt that reads "MIRANDA JULY".
